items.title: Imagine if you had done what the Colts did
items.summary: In unsurprising news, the Indianapolis Colts declined Anthony Richardson’s fifth-year option. The former fourth overall pick is set to be an unrestricted free agent next year which marks the final nail in the coffin. With no trade suitors, the Colts are prepared to run out the clock. This is the reality of the situation. The […]

items.title: Anonymous NFL execs reportedly weigh in on Colts 2026 NFL Draft class
items.summary: According to The Athletic’s Mike Sando, anonymous NFL executives recently weighed in on the Indianapolis Colts 2026 draft class, which even without a first round pick, saw the Horseshoe potentially land two immediate defensive starters on ‘Day 2’: “They would not have gotten a Sauce Gardner picking where they were going to pick, whether that […]

items.title: From Center Grove to Columbus, Caden Curry's high school coach knows he's ready
items.summary: Eric Moore, head football coach at Center Grove High School, spoke to Matt Taylor about coaching the Colts' sixth-round pick at CGHS.

items.title: Rookie paydays slotted for Colts’ draft picks
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The signings are trickling in, which soon will be replaced by a deluge. The days of protracted negotiations with draft picks are a distant memory. Using recent history as a blueprint, the Indianapolis Colts will have most, if not all, of their 2026 draft class under contract in the next week or two. [...]

items.title: Colts decline Anthony Richardson Sr’s fifth-year option
items.summary: The Colts have officially declined the fifth-year option on quarterback Anthony Richardson, a move that had felt inevitable for weeks but still marks a significant moment for the franchise. Richardson, the fourth overall pick in the 2023 NFL Draft out of Florida, will now enter the final year of his rookie contract in 2026 and […]

items.title: No fifth-year option for Colts’ QB Anthony Richardson Sr.
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts won’t trigger the fifth-year option of quarterback Anthony Richardson, according to a source. And that isn’t a surprise. The deadline for teams to exercise the fifth-year option for 2023 first-round picks is Friday, and there will be no action from Indy. At the end of the NFL draft Saturday, general [...]

items.title: Colts Blue Zone Podcast 463: Meet the Newest Colts
items.summary: The 2026 NFL Draft is in the books, and the Indianapolis Colts went a long way in addressing some of their top needs. Dave Griffiths, Mike Chappell and Matt Adams look at the eight players in this year's class and the impact they could have on the upcoming season.

items.title: Colts waive DE Viliami Fehoko Jr., LB Joseph Vaughn; release G Bill Murray; waive-injured T Jack Wilson

items.title: Following NFL Draft, trade suitors for Colts QB Anthony Richardson Sr. may be dwindling
items.summary: When asked about the status for both veteran cornerback Kenny Moore II and quarterback Anthony Richardson Sr., Indianapolis Colts longtime general manager Chris Ballard recently responded, “Nothing. Nothing yet,” in regards to finding a potential trade that would be worth the franchise’s while. Both players had previously requested a trade out of Indianapolis earlier this […]

items.title: Colts need their solid draft to turn into strong production
items.summary: Chris Ballard and the Indianapolis Colts are getting solid marks for their 2026 draft. They filled immediate needs and bolstered their depth across some weaker areas. They appear to have avoided major reaches and got great value with some of their selections like C.J. Allen, A.J. Haulcy, and Deion Burks to name a few. It […]

items.title: Colts announce mascot lineup for "Blue's Birthday Bash" on May 15
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts will once again host Blue’s Birthday Bash, presented by Everwise Credit Union, at the world-renowned Children’s Museum of Indianapolis, celebrating the birthday of four-time NFL Mascot of the Year “Blue.”

items.title: Colts should consider signing 49ers free agent WR Jauan Jennings
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts still arguably need an outside WR2 upgrade, and one noticeable name still remains unsigned, San Francisco 49ers free agent wideout Jauan Jennings. The 28-year-old wide receiver recently visited the Minnesota Vikings, and while no deal was reached, it seems like only a matter of time before the veteran wideout finds a new […]

items.title: Vikings sign Colts backup free agent defensive tackle
items.summary: On Wednesday, the Minnesota Vikings announced that they signed Indianapolis Colts free agent defensive tackle Eric Johnson II, who was an unrestricted free agent, and will not count toward compensatory draft pick consideration: Originally a 2022 5th round pick of the Colts, the 27-year-old defensive tackle appeared in 51 career games for Indianapolis, recording 41 […]

items.title: Could the Colts find a different roster option for Anthony Richardson?
items.summary: What is the best course of action when you have a player that is currently making more money than what he brings in value to your team?  The options always seem to be to either trade the player, or to cut the player.  You try to minimize the loss, by getting rid of the player […]

items.title: Colts projected to gain 2 extra picks in 2027 Draft
items.summary: The over-month long period of the offseason where signings factor into the compensatory pick formula has came and went. After the April 27th deadline, all signings going forward in the offseason have no bearing on the compensatory picks a team receives. Teams that lose more compensatory Free Agents than they acquire are eligible to receive […]

items.title: Colts gave Seth McGowan second chance after 2021 arrest nearly cost him everything
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The incident was addressed every time Seth McGowan met with a prospective employer. That included when he shared time with the Indianapolis Colts. “We vetted hard,’’ general manager Chris Ballard said. McGowan’s persistent plea: I made a serious mistake, but I learned from it. You can trust me. Finally, McGowan’s long wait during [...]

items.title: Indianapolis Colts and Eleven launch program to help Hoosier small businesses connect & grow
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts have teamed up with Eleven Sports Media to launch a brand‑new Small Business Partner Program, creating an unprecedented opportunity for small and medium‑sized businesses across Indiana to connect, grow, and align with the team.

The draft added Zaire Franklin’s successor at middle linebacker [...]

items.title: 10 Colts Things on 2026 NFL Draft: CJ Allen brings toughness & leadership, how A.J. Haulcy could help Lou Anarumo's versatile coverages, Jalen Farmer boosts offensive line & how Caden Curry stayed in Indy
items.summary: The Colts made eight selections in the 2026 NFL Draft, from CJ Allen in the second round to Deion Burks in the seventh round. Here's how each player could impact the Colts and the process it took for Chris Ballard to land them.

The search is exhaustive to uncover the next Gary Brackett, Dominic Rhodes, Joe Reitz, Jack Doyle, Kenny Moore II and so many others who overcame the temporary setback of not being drafted to carve out a solid NFL career. items.title: A capsule look at the Colts’ 8 draft picks
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — The Indianapolis Colts injected eight draft picks into their roster, including two projected starters this week. Second-round pick CJ Allen, a linebacker out of Georgia, and third-rounder A.J. Haulcy, an LSU safety, bring immediate impact potential. That’s especially true with Allen, who’ll replaced Zaire Franklin and run the defense from the Mike position. [...]

items.title: No trades for Colts’ Anthony Richardson Sr., Kenny Moore II
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — Post-draft, it’s status quo with two prominent Indianapolis Colts. The three-day, seven-round, 257-pick NFL Draft ended Saturday evening without quarterback Anthony Richardson Sr. and cornerback Kenny Moore II being traded. That would have been an ideal time for the Colts to resolve trade requests two players submitted. In Richardson’s instance, no team was [...]

items.title: Colts’ Chris Ballard: We got younger, faster on defense
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The major renovation on defense picked up steam Friday night. After a trade-back with Pittsburgh in round 2 – No. 47 overall to No. 53 – the Indianapolis Colts found Zaire Franklin’s successor at middle linebacker with Georgia’s CJ Allen. In round 3, they invested the 78th overall pick in LSU safety A.J. Haulcy. [...]

items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Sauce Gardner isn’t buying into the notion that the Indianapolis Colts don’t have a pick in the first round of Thursday night’s NFL Draft. “I’m the first-round pick,’’ he said this week with a smile. Actually, more than that. “I’m the first-round pick two times,’’ Gardner said. This year, and in 2027. That [...]

items.title: Colts bring back 'Schedule Challenge' contest
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS -- You can take your best guess at predicting the Indianapolis Colts' 2026 schedule for a chance to win tickets to the Super Bowl. That's right--the "Schedule Challenge" contest is back for another go. This year, the contestant with the most accurate guess gets tickets to Super Bowl LXI at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, [...]

items.title: Colts announce '2026 Schedule Challenge'
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts today announced the “2026 Schedule Challenge,” where one contestant can win two tickets to Super Bowl LXI by most accurately predicting the Colts’ entire 18-week schedule for the 2026 regular season.

items.title: 'Making The Team' to recap the road to 2026 Colts Cheer Squad
